findings.title: hasFindingsMin never downgrades on re-review
findings.description: Both `recordFinding` and `completeReview` only ever upgrade `hasFindingsMin` (they check `newOrder > currentOrder`). This means if a PR's most severe finding was `critical` from review #1, and review #2 has only `low` findings, `hasFindingsMin` remains `critical` forever. There is no online path to downgrade. If findings are later marked `irrelevant`, the denormalised `hasFindingsMin` becomes stale. This matters for the `hasFindingsMin` filter on listPullRequests — PRs with all-irrelevant findings still appear as if they have severe findings.

findings.title: stats.findingCount never written to parent PR
findings.description: `completeReview` updates `stats.reviewCount` on the parent PR but never updates `stats.findingCount`. Similarly, `recordFinding` does not touch the parent's stats. The `PullRequestStats.findingCount` field will always remain `null` (its default from `upsertPR`). If the dashboard or downstream consumers read `pr.stats.findingCount`, they'll get stale data.

findings.title: Misindented comment inside if block
findings.description: The comment "Path scoping makes cross-org access impossible" is at the same indentation level as the `if (!pr) {` statement, placing it visually outside the block even though it's between the opening brace and `res.status(404)`. This is misleading — the comment should be indented one level deeper to sit inside the block, matching the pattern used in the GET route.

findings.title: deleteSourceTree batch may exceed Firestore 500 ops
findings.description: `deleteSourceTree` batches all finding deletes + the parent review doc delete into a single Firestore batch without checking the 500-operation limit. The comment says ~10 docs per PR but there's no guard if a legacy review had more than 499 findings — the batch.commit() would fail with a Firestore error.

findings.title: dry-run recompute skips stats counter
findings.description: In dry-run mode, `recomputeParentPr` logs the planned update but does NOT increment `stats.parentPrsRecomputed`. The summary output reports `parent PRs recomputed: 0` even though recomputes would have happened. The log message still prints, but the counter is misleading for operators verifying dry-run output.

findings.title: Findings severity read outside tx in completeReview
findings.description: In `completeReview`, all findings for the review are fetched via a non-transactional read, and the computed `worstSeverity` is later used inside `runTransaction` to update the PR's denormalised `hasFindingsMin` field. A finding created between the external read and the transaction would not be reflected in `hasFindingsMin`, leaving it stale until the next `recordFinding` call corrects it. The comment explains this is intentional (transactions limit reads), and `recordFinding` independently updates the field, so the race is bounded.

findings.title: clearOrgPRs batch can exceed Firestore 500 ops
findings.description: `clearOrgPRs` accumulates all deletes (PR docs + review subdocs + finding subdocs) into a single `WriteBatch`. Firestore batches are capped at 500 operations. If a test seeds many PRs with reviews and findings, the batch could silently exceed the limit and throw. Unlikely in current tests but will bite if test data grows.

findings.title: Dead code: listPRs has zero callers
findings.description: The `listPRs` method is an exact functional subset of `listPullRequests` (both do Firestore query + pagination on the same collection). All callers (code-review-routes.ts) use `listPullRequests`. `listPRs` has zero external callers and should be removed to avoid maintenance confusion.

findings.title: Dead code: getPR/getPRByNumber duplicate getPullRequest
findings.description: After the refactor, `getPR(orgId, prId)` and `getPullRequest(orgId, prId)` have identical implementations. All callers (routes, findings-routes) use `getPullRequest`. `getPR` is only called by `getPRByNumber` which itself has zero external callers. Both should be removed.

findings.title: Stale IDOR comment implies orgId comparison
findings.description: The comment says "IDOR guard: load the PR and verify orgId matches" but the actual check is now just `if (!pr)` — path scoping handles tenant isolation implicitly. The comment text implies an orgId comparison that no longer occurs.

findings.title: review.id undefined for legacy docs in backfill
findings.description: The backfill reads `review.id` from the document body (`reviewDoc.data() as CodeReview`), but the comment in `recomputeParentPr` explicitly notes that legacy review docs may not have the `id` field populated. If `id` is absent, `reviewId` becomes `undefined`, the `!reviewId` guard fires, and the review is silently skipped with only an error log. The `latestReviewId` computation at line ~212 already uses `reviewDoc.id` for this exact reason, but the main migration loop does not.

findings.title: pr.repository.fullName not guarded like other fields
findings.description: The component uses optional chaining for `pr.author?.username` and nullish coalescing for `pr.title`, `pr.headBranch`, `pr.baseBranch`, and `pr.stats.reviewCount` — but `pr.repository.fullName` has no such guard. If the API ever returns an incomplete or legacy-shape payload during rollout, this will throw rather than gracefully degrading.

findings.title: Cross-org cleanup skipped on assertion failure
findings.description: The cross-org test seeds data under OTHER_ORG but calls `clearOrgPRs(OTHER_ORG)` at the end of the test body, not in a `finally` block. If any assertion throws, cleanup is skipped. `beforeEach` only clears `ORG_ID`, so stale OTHER_ORG data persists into subsequent runs, potentially causing flaky test failures.

findings.title: copyFindings does N+1 get-then-set per finding
findings.description: For each finding, `copyFindings` does `await targetRef.get()` to check existence then `await targetRef.set()` to write — two sequential round-trips per finding. For a review with many findings, this is an N+1 pattern. Could be collapsed into a single batch write with existence checked via a collection get.
